_sources.discrepancies: Wisconsin has a strict photo ID requirement per NCSL. The Wisconsin Elections Commission (WEC) confirms a wide range of acceptable photo IDs including Wisconsin ID cards, driver licenses, military IDs, tribal IDs, and student IDs from Wisconsin accredited schools. Voters without ID may receive a free Wisconsin voter ID from the DMV. Wisconsin primary is the second Tuesday in August per statute. Same-day registration is available in Wisconsin through Election Day.

earlyVoting.notes: In-person absentee voting available at municipal clerk offices starting 14 business days before the primary. Weekend hours vary. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
In-person absentee voting begins 14 business days before Election Day at the municipal clerk's office. Weekend hours required in final weeks. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.

In-person absentee voting at municipal clerk offices begins 14 business days before Election Day. Weekend hours required in the two weeks before the election. Same-day registration available through Election Day. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.

acceptedIds: Wisconsin driver's license or state ID card (including expired within 2 years)
U.S. military ID with expiration date or issuance date within 2 years
Student ID from a Wisconsin accredited institution (with signature and expiration date)
Employee ID from a Wisconsin government employer
Wisconsin voter ID card (free from the DMV)
votingRules.expirationRule: IDs must generally not be expired more than 2 years. Student IDs must not be expired. Wisconsin driver's licenses and state IDs may be expired up to 2 years. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
votingRules.impedimentDeclaration: Voters without acceptable ID may obtain a free Wisconsin voter ID card from any DMV service center before Election Day.
votingRules.phonesAtPollsDetail: Wisconsin allows phones at polling places but prohibits photography that reveals how a voter marked their ballot. No electioneering within 100 feet of the polling place entrance.
additionalRules: Open primary: voters do not register by party; voters choose which party's primary ballot to vote on election day.
Same-day voter registration available on Election Day with proof of residence.
No runoffs in Wisconsin — the candidate with the most votes wins.
Free photo ID is available from any Wisconsin DMV service center.

eligibility: Any registered voter may request an absentee ballot without providing a reason
voteByMail.applicationDeadline: 5 p.m. on the Thursday before the election for online/mail requests; in-person absentee available through the Sunday before the election
voteByMail.returnDeadline: Must be received by the municipal clerk by 8 p.m. on Election Day
voteByMail.returnDeadlinePlain: Must arrive at the municipal clerk by 8 p.m. on Election Day. Postmarks do not count.

primaryParticipation.ruleExplanationEn: Wisconsin has an open primary (Wis. Stat. §5.60). Any registered voter may vote in either party's primary. Voters may not split their vote between parties — they must vote on a single party's ballot.
primaryParticipation.ruleExplanationEs: Wisconsin tiene una primaria abierta (Wis. Stat. §5.60). Cualquier votante registrado puede votar en la primaria de cualquier partido. Los votantes no pueden dividir su voto entre partidos: deben votar en la boleta de un solo partido.
